title: Boat Rental in Langkawi: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Langkawi, Malaysia, offers access to the Andaman Sea’s emerald waters and limestone karsts. Depart from Kuah Jetty or Telaga Harbour Park, with bareboat and skippered charters starting from around MYR 450 for a two-hour shared tour. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local operators to find a skippered charter that suits your group and budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Langkawi?

Prices for boat rental in Langkawi range from MYR 250 per person for a shared tour to MYR 8,000 for a full-day crewed yacht. A small motorboat for half a day typically costs MYR 600, 1,000, while a 42-foot catamaran for a full day with skipper starts around MYR 3,500. These rates usually include fuel, crew and basic insurance. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours): from MYR 250 per person
  • Small motorboat (half day, up to 6 guests): MYR 600, 1,000
  • Catamaran (full day, up to 12 guests): from MYR 3,500
  • Crewed yacht (full day, up to 10 guests): MYR 5,000, 8,000

Where to set off in Langkawi

Most charters depart from Kuah Jetty, Telaga Harbour Park, or the Royal Langkawi Yacht Club in Pantai Kok. Other popular launch points include Cenang Beach jetty and the smaller marina at Tanjung Rhu. For private yacht charters and the Princess V58 for hire, you will likely depart from the well-serviced Telaga Harbour Park, which offers full marina facilities and easy access to the Andaman Sea. One option worth a look is the Princess V58 for hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat, best for island-hopping to the Langkawi archipelago’s nearby islets; suitable for 4, 6 guests; from MYR 600 per half day.
  2. Catamaran, ideal for stable cruising and snorkelling trips to Pulau Payar; can accommodate 10, 12 guests; from MYR 3,500 per full day.
  3. Sailboat, perfect for a relaxed day sail around the Andaman Sea; suits 4, 8 guests; from MYR 2,000 per half day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, the Princess V58 for hire at Royal Langkawi Yacht Club offers premium comfort for up to 8 guests; from MYR 6,000 per full day.
  5. Traditional wooden boat (perahu), a local experience for up to 12 guests; from MYR 800 per half day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Langkawi?

If you rent a bareboat (no skipper), you need a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a Malaysian boat licence; operators confirm exact requirements. For small powerboats under 15 horsepower, no licence is required in Langkawi, but you must be over 18. Most visitors opt for a skippered charter, which avoids licence concerns completely. You can also see more at check availability.

Local tip: the northeast monsoon from November to March brings calm seas and steady winds, making it the best time to charter a boat in Langkawi, especially around the sheltered waters of Kilim Karst Geoforest Park.

At a glance

  • Price range: MYR 250, 8,000 per day
  • Peak season: November to March
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on a catamaran
  • Local currency: MYR (Malaysian Ringgit)
  • Booking platform: BoatHire24

Questions about boat rental in Langkawi

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Langkawi?

Yes, if you hold an ICC or Malaysian licence, you can hire a bareboat from operators at Telaga Harbour Park.

What is the best month for boat rental in Langkawi?

November to March offers the calmest conditions, with light winds from the northeast and sunny skies.

Are there hidden fees in the boat rental price?

Most quotes include fuel, crew and insurance, but confirm the cancellation policy and any park fees for Pulau Payar.

How do I book a boat in Langkawi for 2026?

You can browse and compare verified boats directly on BoatHire24’s marketplace, then book through the platform with secure payment.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Langkawi and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
